config.title: Berlin Marathon 2026: FAQ
config.subtitle: Straight answers on the date, what closes and when it reopens, how to cross the course, where to watch, and what marathon weekend does to an ordinary Berlin trip.

items.a: The Berlin Marathon 2026 takes place on Sunday 27 September 2026. The handbike top field starts at 08:20, elite wheelchair racers at 08:26, and the remaining wheelchair and handbike field at 08:29. Runners leave Straße des 17. Juni in six waves at 08:45, 09:05, 09:33, 10:02, 10:12 and 10:27. Saturday 26 September includes a 5K from Potsdamer Platz at 10:00, a mini marathon and the inline skating event from 12:20.

items.q: Which parts of Berlin are closed for the marathon?
items.a: The course is a 42 kilometre loop through the middle of the city, so closures reach Tiergarten, Mitte, Friedrichshain, Kreuzberg, Neukölln, Schöneberg, Steglitz, Wilmersdorf and Zehlendorf. The heaviest and longest closures are in Tiergarten and Mitte, because that is where the start, the finish and the final kilometres are. The exact 2026 closure map is published by the organiser and by Berlin’s traffic information centre in September.

items.q: What time do the roads reopen after the Berlin Marathon?
items.a: They do not all reopen together, and they do not reopen when the winner finishes. In the traffic plan for the 2025 race, Friedrichshain and Kreuzberg were scheduled from around 14:00, Tempelhof-Schöneberg from about 16:30, Mitte and Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf from roughly 17:30, and Unter den Linden at about 19:00. Straße des 17. Juni stayed closed beyond race day while the finish area was dismantled. Use those times only to understand the shape of the day; wait for the official 2026 map before relying on a street reopening.

items.q: How do I cross the marathon course?
items.a: A short U-Bahn or S-Bahn journey, or a station underpass, is usually the practical way past a blocked section. Do not assume every service is normal or that every line runs beneath the whole course: check BVG, VBB or S-Bahn live service before you leave. In 2025 the marked surface crossings included points near Rankestraße, Wittenbergplatz, Potsdamer Platz and Friedrichstraße, but those are not confirmed 2026 promises. Around the start and finish area, the organiser’s current spectator guidance directs people across at Pariser Platz only. Do not try to walk around the full course, because it loops.

items.q: Do buses and trams run during the Berlin Marathon?
items.a: Some routes continue with diversions, while others may be shortened or temporarily suspended. The pattern can change as police release sections of the course. Check the live journey planner close to departure. U-Bahn and S-Bahn services are usually the stronger first choice for crossing the city, but they also need a live service check.

items.q: Where is the best place to watch the Berlin Marathon?
items.a: Go west and go late. The Memorial Church sits at about km 35 and Potsdamer Platz at km 38.5, and by the time the main field reaches them the crowd has thinned enough to get to the front of the barrier. The Reichstag at around km 6.5 and Friedrichstadtpalast at km 8 fill up early while the field is still dense, and the Brandenburg Gate finish at km 42 is the most crowded point of the whole day.

items.q: Does the Berlin Marathon affect Saturday as well?
items.a: Yes. The 5K starts from Potsdamer Platz at 10:00 and follows the final five kilometres, while the inline skating event starts from 12:20 on a multi-lap city circuit rather than the runners’ full marathon route. In 2025 that skating circuit affected Straße des 17. Juni, Großer Stern, Ernst-Reuter-Platz, Otto-Suhr-Allee, Hofjägerallee, Reichpietschufer, Potsdamer Straße, Leipziger Straße, Jerusalemer Straße, Markgrafenstraße, Französische Straße, Glinkastraße and Unter den Linden. Check the 2026 Saturday map and avoid planning a midday journey across the circuit.

items.q: Is the Tiergarten closed before the marathon?
items.a: The park is not automatically closed, but the start and finish build can affect the central axis well before race day. In 2025, Straße des 17. Juni between Yitzhak-Rabin-Straße and the Brandenburg Gate was closed from 13 to 24 September, with Großer Stern and Yitzhak-Rabin-Straße joining from 17 September. Those dates are historical, not a 2026 timetable. Check this year’s build notice before visiting the Victory Column or planning a walk along the central axis.

items.q: Can I still visit museums and sights on marathon Sunday?
items.a: Many venues may keep their normal Sunday opening hours, but their entrances and the journey to them can still be affected. Check the individual venue, the live transport route and the closure map. Pick one cluster on one side of the course rather than stacking timed bookings across Berlin. The 2025 plan reopened Friedrichshain and Kreuzberg from around 14:00, but confirm the 2026 barriers before moving.

items.q: How many people run the Berlin Marathon?
items.a: It is one of the World Marathon Majors and one of the largest marathons in the world. For 2025, the organiser expected 55,146 runners and nearly 80,000 participants across the weekend from 160 nations; those are not final 2026 entry or finisher counts. Berlin’s traffic centre expected roughly one million spectators along the course. The flat course is known for fast times: Eliud Kipchoge set the course record of 2:01:09 in 2022, and Sabastian Sawe won in 2:02:16 in 2025.
